question.text: Under Rule 19(2)(b) of SCRR 1957, what is the minimum public offer requirement for a company with post-issue capital of Rs. 3,000 crore?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For post-issue capital exceeding Rs. 1,600 crore but not exceeding Rs. 4,000 crore, the minimum public offer must be equivalent to Rs. 400 crore. Rs. 3,000 crore falls in this bracket. **The Trap:** 25% applies only when post-issue capital is Rs. 1,600 crore or less. Candidates often default to the 25% rule without checking the slab. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX tests these slabs extensively; NISM Series V-B (MF Foundation) touches on listing norms indirectly. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Memorize the four slabs — Rs. 1,600 crore / Rs. 4,000 crore / Rs. 1 lakh crore — and the corresponding minimum offer requirements for quick recall.
